_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6)**_

Here's how to run Cal.com locally:

## First-time setup

1. **Install dependencies** (if you haven't already):
```bash
yarn
```

2. **Set up your `.env` file** — it looks like you already have one open. Make sure it's properly configured (database URL, etc.).

3. **Run with database setup** (recommended for first run):
```bash
yarn dx
```
This handles database setup + starts the dev server together.

## Subsequent runs

Just start the dev server:
```bash
yarn dev
```

The app runs at `http://localhost:3000` by default.

## Other useful options

| Command | What it does |
|---|---|
| `yarn dev` | Web app only |
| `yarn dev:all` | Web + website + console |
| `yarn dev:api` | Web + API proxy |
| `yarn dx` | Dev + database setup |

## Database (if needed separately)

```bash
yarn db-seed    # Seed with test data
yarn db-studio  # Open Prisma Studio UI
```

If you run into issues with missing types after schema changes, run `yarn prisma generate` first.
